name: =?utf-8?Q?Illinois=20Review?=
subject: SUNDAY ALERT: IL GOP Donor Update
body:
The list of senior officials of the Republican Party of Illinois who have never financially contributed to the GOP is shocking

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/61331f9a0358/sunday-alert-il-gop-donor-update?e=1cb5fdae9e)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

According to Records, IL GOP Leaders Give Very Little Money, or Not at All (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/according-to-records-il-gop-leaders-give-very-little-money-or-not-at-all.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

On Thursday, Illinois Review (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/gop-state-central-committees-donation-problem-most-dont-donate-to-the-il-gop-despite-kolber-belittling-grassroots.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) reported that nearly half of the IL GOP state central committee members haven’t donated to the IL GOP, despite serving as senior party officials.

And while many of the state central committee members are upset with this public revelation, not one single person has offered to show any proof that they have in fact donated to the IL GOP.

And since the state central committee and senior IL GOP officials are obsessed with money, Illinois Review is taking a deeper dive into some of these donations – or a lack thereof.

============================================================
** (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/according-to-records-il-gop-leaders-give-very-little-money-or-not-at-all.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
IL GOP leader and Sergeant at Arms Steve Boulton stands ready behind the flags to remove the mic from former US Senate candidate Peggy Hubbard on Dec. 10, 2022. (Photo credit: Illinois Review)

State central committee member Jesus “Jay” Reyes (Dist. 4), an attorney who served as ** Deputy Comptroller (https://www.rblandmark.com/2015/08/11/triton-trustees-welcome-back-riverside-resident-to-board/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
under the late Judy Baar Topinka – records indicate on the ** Illinois State Board of Elections website (https://www.elections.il.gov/CampaignDisclosure/ContributionSearchByAllContributions.aspx?ddlContributionType=wOGh3QTPfKqV2YWjeRmjTeStk426RfVK&ddlState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTime=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTimeThru=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&txtLastOnlyName=rM5VUIQFRnqBNFPMmBTGHg%3d%3d&ddlLastOnlyNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&txtFirstName=jYaSxv5yqAU%2fzPaxFqXTAg%3d%3d&ddlFirstNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&ddlVendorState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
that he’s donated a total of $1,250. And both of his donations were in 2005 – $1,000 to Citizens for Judy Baar Topinka and $250 to the Peoples Economy Party.

A record of
** Open Secrets (https://www.opensecrets.org/donor-lookup/results?name=Jesus+reyes&cycle=&state=IL&zip=&employ=&occupation=&jurisdiction=&cand=&type=&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
indicates Reyes made a single donation in 2005 to Judy Baar Topinka.

And not one donation to the IL GOP – an apparent prerequisite to attend last Saturday’s meeting according to senior
** IL GOP official Vince Kolber (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-treasurer-to-grassroots-during-meeting-if-you-havent-donated-then-why-are-you-here.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
.

State central committee member Joan Lasonde (Dist. 9), the unofficial timekeeper during last Saturday’s meeting where she would yell, “time!” when grassroots speakers’ time was up – records indicate on the
** Illinois State Board of Elections website (https://www.elections.il.gov/CampaignDisclosure/ContributionSearchByAllContributions.aspx?ddlContributionType=wOGh3QTPfKqV2YWjeRmjTeStk426RfVK&ddlState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTime=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTimeThru=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&txtLastOnlyName=8aNtabKOb8vjwHC%2bM7k4DQ%3d%3d&ddlLastOnlyNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&txtFirstName=JyZkweNe5Pe%2bdXdIPBxrTQ%3d%3d&ddlFirstNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&ddlVendorState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
that she’s donated a total of $3,280 since 2013.

Her last donation was August 31, 2022 when she donated $250 to Patriots for Evan Kasal.

In 2018, Lasonde loaned her campaign committee, Joan for Illinois, $1,000.

In 2013, 2015 and 2016, Lasonde made several small donations to the New Trier Republican Organization.

A record of
** Open Secrets (https://www.opensecrets.org/donor-lookup/results?name=Joan+Lasonde&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
indicates Lasonde made four donations going back to 2013, totaling $1,500.

In 2016 and 2018, she made donations to herself.

And not one donation to the IL GOP – an apparent prerequisite to attend last Saturday’s meeting according to senior
** IL GOP official Vince Kolber (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-treasurer-to-grassroots-during-meeting-if-you-havent-donated-then-why-are-you-here.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
.

State central committee member Laura Pollastrini (Dist. 11), who took over as GOP Treasurer – records indicate on the
** Illinois State Board of Elections website (https://www.elections.il.gov/CampaignDisclosure/ContributionSearchByAllContributions.aspx?ddlContributionType=wOGh3QTPfKqV2YWjeRmjTeStk426RfVK&ddlState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTime=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTimeThru=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&txtLastOnlyName=XSBnV%2b6JOHNP07NBdBEEKoET4T7b9BIy&ddlLastOnlyNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&txtFirstName=zxu4cBneTV51n2op9X5Dvw%3d%3d&ddlFirstNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&ddlVendorState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
that she’s donated a total of $2,715.87 since 1994.

Her last donation was a $2,000 loan to herself on February 29, 2012.

In 2006, Pallastrini donated twice to Citizens for Pamela Fenner totaling $175.

In 1994, Pallastrini donated twice to the James Pate Philip Campaign Fund totaling $531.87.

A record of
** Open Secrets (https://www.opensecrets.org/donor-lookup/results?name=Laura+Pollastrini&cycle=&state=IL&zip=&employ=&occupation=&jurisdiction=&cand=&type=&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
indicates Pallastrini made a total of four donations going back to 2006 totaling $2,175.

In 2012, Pallastrini donated $2,000 to herself, and in 2006 she made two donations totaling $175 to Pamela Joy Fenner.

And not one donation to the IL GOP – an apparent prerequisite to attend last Saturday’s meeting according to senior
** IL GOP official Vince Kolber (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-treasurer-to-grassroots-during-meeting-if-you-havent-donated-then-why-are-you-here.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
.

Records indicate that Chicago GOP chair Steve Boulton, and Sergeant at Arms during last Saturday’s state central committee meeting – an appointment made by embattled chairman Don Tracy, where he spent the day harassing, intimidating and yelling at the grassroots attendees – even surrounding a speaker when their time was up during public comments.

Donation records show that Boulton hasn’t donated since 2013.

According to the
** Illinois State Board of Elections website (https://www.elections.il.gov/CampaignDisclosure/ContributionSearchByAllContributions.aspx?ddlContributionType=wOGh3QTPfKqV2YWjeRmjTeStk426RfVK&ddlState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTime=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&ddlFiledDateTimeThru=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&txtLastOnlyName=MZAGTwXGNcMgBFD1rdfPyw%3d%3d&ddlLastOnlyNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&txtFirstName=lPhRCqmIo2f0KZ91emTviQ%3d%3d&ddlFirstNameSearchType=uhV70GVj7rs2mYAJ0IPVG%2fPXF28eNgo%2f&ddlVendorState=Ry707kcsXsM%3d&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
, Boulton has donated a total of $1,425 since 2010. Three campaign contributions to the Dan Rutherford Campaign Committee in 2013, and one donation to the Mark Thompson Campaign Committee in 2010.

A record of
** Open Secrets (https://www.opensecrets.org/donor-lookup/results?name=Steve+Boulton&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
also indicates a total of three donations – all going to Rutherford in 2013 totaling $425.

And not one donation to the IL GOP – an apparent prerequisite to attend last Saturday’s meeting according to senior
** IL GOP official Vince Kolber (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/il-gop-treasurer-to-grassroots-during-meeting-if-you-havent-donated-then-why-are-you-here.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
.

And as for
** Mr. Kolber (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2022/12/inside-the-il-gops-obsession-with-money-and-protecting-chairman-don-tracy.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
– instead of addressing his belittling comments to the more than 200 grassroots conservatives in the room at last Saturday’s meeting, perhaps he should have faced his fellow state central committee members instead, and asked,

“If you haven’t donated to the IL GOP, then what are you doing here?”

It’s no wonder the IL GOP can’t win elections or raise any money – their own senior leaders don’t even support the party.

** (https://youtu.be/cs02JRLbp0g?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Mark Vargas and Scott Kaspar discuss the two most shocking moments at the Dec. 10, 2022 State Central Committee meeting, on "Mark My Words" on AM 560 (Dec. 11, 2022)

To learn more about Illinois Review, visit ** www.illinoisreview.com (https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=007b9b59b4-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_12_17_08_13&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-007b9b59b4-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Hello Friend, As 2022 comes to an end, I am thinking about how I can help you make 2023 a landmark year in your Christian walk. I want to help you grow closer to Jesus all year long, so that you, in turn, can impact the world around you. That's why I've written the brand-new 2023 _Pathway to Victory_ Daily Devotional. It contains 260 devotions (Monday through Friday for all 52 weeks) written to encourage, inspire, convict, comfort, and teach you biblical truth so that you can face each day with spiritual readiness. Here is an outline of what we'll be studying together if you choose to follow along:
---
* From January to February, we'll begin a spiritual fitness program using wisdom from the book of Hebrews.
* From March through May, we'll learn how to walk with God by faith in our everyday lives by studying the life of Abraham.
* In the summer, we'll gain a biblical understanding of grace, and discover the joy and freedom of living obediently.
* And in the fall and winter, we'll meditate together on the parables of Jesus!

---

subject: Happy Hanukkah!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc
body:
The holiday season is upon us | | |
---
---
Peter, Tonight, Americans from across our country are coming together to celebrate the first night of Hanukkah. No matter which holiday you celebrate, this season is a time for family and fellowship. Now’s the time to tell loved ones how much you love them and how thankful you are to have them in your life. As we listen to holiday songs & unwrap gifts in the coming days, let us all take a moment to reflect on this special time of year & all that it brings. Thank you for your continued support,
---
| |
---
|
---
| | Katie Britt
U.S. Senator-elect for Alabama

name: Jenn McClellan
subject: Now is your moment.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
a lot of people don’t know there’s an election on Tuesday | | |
---
Friend, I’m not going to waste your time. Election Day is on Tuesday. If you were _waiting_ for the most critical part of this campaign to donate, **today is that day**. In another week, your donation won’t change anything, but if you **[split just $5 between my campaign and Maggie Hassan's right now,](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56126493/383969349/-1143236768?refcode=mh-fr-20221218&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjAyODg2Yi0xNTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=o95iQo4SZFj8cOFRnj8fy0w1e-MQakrG3nHUbFvtwT8=)** we can use it to get our supporters across the district to the polls. | | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56126494/383969350/-1143236768?refcode=mh-fr-20221218&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjAyODg2Yi0xNTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=o95iQo4SZFj8cOFRnj8fy0w1e-MQakrG3nHUbFvtwT8=)
---
I’ve been talking to our field team and our volunteers, and here’s the truth: a lot of people don’t know that there’s an election on Tuesday.
**That’s why we’re working so hard to run ads on digital and radio, place robocalls, print flyers, and more. We’re doing everything we can to make sure our voters know where, when, and how to cast their ballots.**
Grassroots supporters power this voter outreach and thus power our success. **[Can you split a donation between my campaign and Maggie Hassan's before it’s too late?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/56126495/383969351/-1143236768?refcode=mh-fr-20221218&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYjAyODg2Yi0xNTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=o95iQo4SZFj8cOFRnj8fy0w1e-MQakrG3nHUbFvtwT8=)**

clean_body: a lot of people don’t know there’s an election on Tuesday | | | Friend, I’m not going to waste your time. Election Day is on Tuesday. If you were waiting for the most critical part of this campaign to donate, today is that day. In another week, your donation won’t change anything, but if you = we can use it to get our supporters across the district to the polls. | | = I’ve been talking to our field team and our volunteers, and here’s the truth: a lot of people don’t know that there’s an election on Tuesday. That’s why we’re working so hard to run ads on digital and radio, place robocalls, print flyers, and more. We’re doing everything we can to make sure our voters know where, when, and how to cast their ballots. Grassroots supporters power this voter outreach and thus power our success. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = = We’re all counting on you, Jenn McClellan Candidate for Congress, VA-04 | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-2021-footer?refcode=emailheader&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-2021-footer?refcode=emailheader&amount=25]
HORRIBLE: The Georgia GOP passed a law to restrict Vote-by-Mail – slashing the number of voters in the Georgia Run-off Election [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1]
URGENT POLL → [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1] Do you support legislation to guarantee Vote-by-Mail in all 50 states? [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1]YES >> [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1] NO >> [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1]
Here’s the deal.
There was a VERY important run-off election on December 6th in Georgia that EXPANDED the Democratic Senate Majority.
But Republicans in Georgia and other states strategically pass bills to restrict voters in a pathetic attempt to maintain or expand their power.
Because they know when Democrats are able to easily vote, Democrats win.
We think there should be a federal bill to guarantee Vote-by-Mail in EVERY state. Not just because it’s good for Democrats, but because it’s good for democracy.
That’s why we’re reaching out to Ohio Democrats to see if they agree. We want to see if there’s massive public support for National Vote-by-Mail legislation. We’ll have to submit the results of this poll into the record, so we need your completed response by 11:59PM.
Do you support legislation to guarantee Vote-by-Mail in all 50 states? [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1]YES >> [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1] NO >> [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1]
Team DASS [ https://action.demsofstate.org/a/vbm-poll-nov22-1]
DASS is a national organization supporting the election of Democratic Secretaries of State who are committed to protecting and preserving voting rights and ballot access for all Americans through fair, honest, open, and accurate elections.
Help us fight back against the GOP's voter suppression agenda by pitching in today!
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-2021-footer?refcode=footer-button&amount=25]

name: Build Alaska's Future
subject: Invest in 2023 and double your impact!
body:
| | |
---
| | Dear Friend, As the year winds down, we ask that you consider giving to [**Build Alaska’s Future,**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56128706/384007541/755459924?hosted_button_id=WRTETZ8LLHYYC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODlmZmI1MC00YTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jW1s9WK5FJ3sM9PSjwh1FNor9kGWCabMdsLGYnoLaHU=&emci=31223cbb-397f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&emdi=089ffb50-4a7f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&ceid=9512399) an effort to keep the public policy discourse accurate and transparent, and ensure that Alaskans continue to get the information they need about the benefits of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
---
[INVEST IN 2023!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56128707/384007542/755459924?hosted_button_id=WRTETZ8LLHYYC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODlmZmI1MC00YTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jW1s9WK5FJ3sM9PSjwh1FNor9kGWCabMdsLGYnoLaHU=&emci=31223cbb-397f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&emdi=089ffb50-4a7f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&ceid=9512399)
---
In 2022, [Buildalaska.org](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56128708/384007543/2108661445?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODlmZmI1MC00YTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jW1s9WK5FJ3sM9PSjwh1FNor9kGWCabMdsLGYnoLaHU=&emci=31223cbb-397f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&emdi=089ffb50-4a7f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&ceid=9512399) made significant strides in promoting the benefits of the once-in-a-generation opportunity:
* The robust [org project map](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56128709/384007544/913310059?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODlmZmI1MC00YTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jW1s9WK5FJ3sM9PSjwh1FNor9kGWCabMdsLGYnoLaHU=&emci=31223cbb-397f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&emdi=089ffb50-4a7f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&ceid=9512399) gives Alaskans a tool to find out where infrastructure projects are happening and information on how communities can engage.
* An ongoing series of radio and digital spots contextualized the benefits of federal infrastructure funding with testimonials from workers, Alaska Native voices, and other community leaders. Below are two radio spots published to engage the public on the importance of civil discourse and working together:
* [Marcie Obremski of IBEW](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56128710/384007545/-803653937?v=GsHJ24hMHEs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODlmZmI1MC00YTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jW1s9WK5FJ3sM9PSjwh1FNor9kGWCabMdsLGYnoLaHU=&emci=31223cbb-397f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&emdi=089ffb50-4a7f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&ceid=9512399)
* [Clay Koplin, former mayor of Cordova](https://click.everyaction.com/k/56128711/384007546/660247699?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwODlmZmI1MC00YTdmLWVkMTEtOWQ3YS0wMDBkM2E5ZWI5MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jW1s9WK5FJ3sM9PSjwh1FNor9kGWCabMdsLGYnoLaHU=&emci=31223cbb-397f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&emdi=089ffb50-4a7f-ed11-9d7a-000d3a9eb913&ceid=9512399)

**However** , there’s still work to be done to promote the benefits of bi-partisanship and working together to move America and Alaska forward! clean_body: | | | | | Dear Friend, As the year winds down, we ask that you consider giving to an effort to keep the public policy discourse accurate and transparent, and ensure that Alaskans continue to get the information they need about the benefits of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. In 2022, made significant strides in promoting the benefits of the once-in-a-generation opportunity: The robust gives Alaskans a tool to find out where infrastructure projects are happening and information on how communities can engage. An ongoing series of radio and digital spots contextualized the benefits of federal infrastructure funding with testimonials from workers, Alaska Native voices, and other community leaders. Below are two radio spots published to engage the public on the importance of civil discourse and working together: However , there’s still work to be done to promote the benefits of bi-partisanship and working together to move America and Alaska forward! ** BuildAlaska.org and iHeart are continuing to work together to provide clear and accessible information through radio and digital spots, and iHeart is matching donations dollar-for-dollar up to $100K to fund this campaign through the end of the year! work to do

|

Hey guys,

The other day I was at the Hamilton County Courthouse, where I was dismissed from jury duty. Reason given: orientation for a new job starting. 😂😱

[**_Rush a donation so I can start working for YOU!_**](http://links.jdforohio.org/u/click?_t=52e88da6462b43fd9dbbfea97c7983e9&_m=b8ee3241c2d54c4bb97b684c9b9f4bd1&_e=BOot4jAFTE_38OYnV0m3tG4uOqrvUOCrSVngKy8LU_xbJMODKvkdiCJEPqLnRoHilVku3ftuLr_wNtn7iuSufugE7DL-v_TDiMfjANLJn1J4ecUOq04fIoJFuPCQRbW4-T-ulIrcugIZQ4y-XKOcIkCx3DIAaAyYNzOKNT3iSGarc0tiU9WpM3Virgwg0nswcMhOYW9ef667_A-WC6LMFxEzQWByxFEc3wCKRrSTRRoWa1KgplgCPZSmTV5HLzSvGjImOlgQLAJbSLEHx-py3PsgfFNT5kumEWDKugAOmuVWKeFpW9mftbyMbyf4YbiEcsojeTtLRpZMLAiFj_5isQEK7ze3xjo1p9vFLOI4kS4vHN2w79GY8imc_vzrcGzPSPF2cix_0LLwO8LhIN80NhzxnIxhb3hC2nJ7hznZHHi5SodFOALFEc6bSNadJnmfQp-OLAA48CUZfaXixAQ7DXMjbXcEFvd3j_QJ4g-wucgDd337ylu6jA8G35iJrzOq)
[](http://links.jdforohio.org/u/click?_t=52e88da6462b43fd9dbbfea97c7983e9&_m=b8ee3241c2d54c4bb97b684c9b9f4bd1&_e=BOot4jAFTE_38OYnV0m3tG4uOqrvUOCrSVngKy8LU_xbJMODKvkdiCJEPqLnRoHilVku3ftuLr_wNtn7iuSufugE7DL-v_TDiMfjANLJn1J4ecUOq04fIoJFuPCQRbW4-T-ulIrcugIZQ4y-XKOcIkCx3DIAaAyYNzOKNT3iSGarc0tiU9WpM3Virgwg0nswcMhOYW9ef667_A-WC6LMFxEzQWByxFEc3wCKRrSTRRoWa1KgplgCPZSmTV5HLzSvGjImOlgQLAJbSLEHx-py3PsgfFNT5kumEWDKugAOmuVWKeFpW9mftbyMbyf4YbiEcsojeTtLRpZMLAiFj_5isQEK7ze3xjo1p9vFLOI4kS621xFai8GbK9UAQu8iLFRhLHIordHe7vlC2I7oxvyAVXF9xOurJ2gY2kONrrwZvv2U7SXpkA4Edv2jchWYX2dakMUEL7EpKWgzNf_qDzjPBhXOriizM1bMg9TrWraH6DKONlbTITwSsnHIgmn9vbV1)
Anyways, I am **_THRILLED_** to be your next Senator. You elected me, now it’s time I work for **_YOU._** It’s my job now — literally.

**First order of business:** fight for Conservative values that have shaped our incredible country. And here’s how we’re going to do it:

* Limit the scope of government and cut bureaucratic red tape.
* Crack down on crime and ensure that criminals are off the streets.
* Defend your right to defend yourself and preserve the Second Amendment.
* LOWER TAXES!!!
* Solve the Southern border crisis and reform our legal immigration system
* Combat the drug and opioid epidemic
* Restore American manufacturing and fight for small businesses!

**I’ve got a pretty big laundry list, so the sooner we get to work, the better.**

clean_body: work to do | Hey guys, The other day I was at the Hamilton County Courthouse, where I was dismissed from jury duty. Reason given: orientation for a new job starting. 😂😱 Anyways, I am THRILLED to be your next Senator. You elected me, now it’s time I work for YOU. It’s my job now — literally. First order of business: fight for Conservative values that have shaped our incredible country. And here’s how we’re going to do it: Limit the scope of government and cut bureaucratic red tape. Crack down on crime and ensure that criminals are off the streets. Defend your right to defend yourself and preserve the Second Amendment. LOWER TAXES!!! Solve the Southern border crisis and reform our legal immigration system Combat the drug and opioid epidemic Restore American manufacturing and fight for small businesses! I’ve got a pretty big laundry list, so the sooner we get to work, the better. God bless, JD | Paid for by JD Vance for Senate Inc. JD Vance for Senate Inc., P.O. Box 6564 Cincinnati, OH 45206 Copyright (C) 2022 JD Vance for US Senate, All rights reserved.

subject: We’re missing your survey, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
What should John focus on in the Senate?
#
We’ll keep this short(s) —
We’re missing your survey, Derek! **[Can you take a minute to fill it out and let John know what you want him to focus on while in the U.S. Senate?](https://act.johnfetterman.com/survey/em-priorities-survey-2022/?source=em20221218-8169&t=1&akid=8169%2E434096%2EKHUQlw)**
**[TAKE OUR SURVEY »](https://act.johnfetterman.com/survey/em-priorities-survey-2022/?source=em20221213-8169&t=2&akid=8169%2E434096%2EKHUQlw)**
Just like John ran a campaign centered around folks like you, he’s ready to legislate on behalf of the issues that you and your loved ones face every day.
But we can only do that if we know the issues that are most important to you.
**[Please take one minute today to fill out our priorities survey today.](https://act.johnfetterman.com/survey/em-priorities-survey-2022/?source=em20221218-8169&t=3&akid=8169%2E434096%2EKHUQlw)**
—Team Fetterman

body:
If Congressman Ruben Gallego runs against Senator Sinema, what should VoteVets do?
#
Derek -
As you know Senator Kyrsten Sinema recently decided to leave the Democratic Party and serve as an Independent.
We’re wondering how you feel about that?
And specifically, if Congressman Ruben Gallego runs against her, what should VoteVets do?
This poll is important to us. The result will actually mean something. So please:

# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/12?t=1&akid=15564%2E3422442%2E33vbNt)
Katie’s campaign has always been grassroots powered. We’ve been able to compete with millions in Republican spending and win _without_ taking a dime of corporate PAC money. **Our average contribution this month is just $16.27.**
[****](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14195?refcode=EMAE18_AVG_20221218_V1_F&t=2&akid=15564%2E3422442%2E33vbNt)
And as one of less than a dozen Members of Congress who doesn’t take lobbyist money, Katie relies on–and is so grateful for–the support of working people from across the country.
**[Consider chipping in $16.27 to help power our movement today.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14195?refcode=EMAE18_AVG_20221218_V1_F&t=3&akid=15564%2E3422442%2E33vbNt)**
Thank you!
—Team Porter

Paid for by Katie Porter for Congress
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you would like to update your information, please **[click here.](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/em-update-info-footer/?t=4&akid=15564%2E3422442%2E33vbNt)** Our campaign is powered by grassroots supporters chipping in to emails just like this one. If you would like to receive fewer emails, please [**click here**](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp-unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=15564%2E3422442%2E33vbNt).

To contribute via check, please address to: Katie Porter for Congress, PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
